text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - small caps just had their best first half in 35 years, which tells you risk appetite is genuinely alive. Your chip/tech names are the direct beneficiaries when money is moving into growth. No macro shock today; the Iran/oil noise is a sideshow for your book.

Your holdings: PTNM is down 15.25% and sitting just above its $9.50 stop - on margin, this small spec can hurt fast, watch it closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; margin amplifies any sudden drop, manage size manually. SOXL is 3x leveraged - it's up 3.17% but decays over time; make sure your $200 stop is where you'd actually exit.

Watch: Whether AMAT and SMH can hold their recent momentum given the Sandisk analyst chatter - that's your read on chip sentiment. Also watch if small-cap strength (IWM, TNA setups) broadens into your names.

Bottom line: Your book is in decent shape - the one job today is protecting against the PTNM bleed and keeping SOXL/SPCX sized right on margin.
plays: PTNM is 15% underwater and just $0.89 above your $9.50 stop - on margin this drags fast; consider cutting or sizing down now rather than waiting for the stop to hit.
SOXL: 3x leverage decays daily - if you're up 3.17% and comfortable, tighten your stop toward $220-230 to lock in some cushion without fully exiting.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist (AMAT grade A, entry ~$700-701, stop $685) but no fresh catalyst in today's headlines justifies adding margin exposure right now; watch and wait.
